featured.title: Home Lab Agent Orchestrator
featured.why_it_matters: A dry-run, policy-gated agent architecture for homelab incident diagnosis that blocked unsafe actions while evidence reduction recovered diagnosis quality under noisy logs.
featured.result: Unsafe-action block rate stayed at 1.0 in the tested scenarios; evidence reduction recovered noisy-log diagnosis accuracy to 1.0 in the bounded synthetic fixture set.
featured.bounds: Synthetic incident fixtures, one small local model, and GB10-class hardware; not proof of production generalization.

featured.title: Resource-Bounded Agent Kernel
featured.why_it_matters: Frames autonomous-agent reliability as a kernel/control-plane problem: typed dispatch, quota checks, and OS-level subprocess controls before actions execute.
featured.result: The paper reports enforced resource boundaries while preserving successful completion for in-quota tasks across toy-model and subprocess replay harnesses.
featured.bounds: Harness validation, not a production kernel certification.

featured.title: Evidence-Bound Proof Synthesizer for Tool Ledger
featured.why_it_matters: Explores automatically synthesizing proof objects for tool calls from the evidence already present in agent traces.
featured.result: Generated benchmark: 200/200 benign accepted and 200/200 adversarial refused; real local transcript replay reported 95.2% checker/trace acceptance with policy-bounded refusals.
featured.bounds: Validated on local transcript corpora and generated traces only.

featured.title: DFlash vLLM/SGLang Throughput Shootout
featured.why_it_matters: A concrete GB10 serving benchmark that treats performance tuning as part of the research loop, including negative compatibility evidence.
featured.result: Reported vLLM speculative-decoding throughput speedups from 1.61x to 4.17x across prompt sets with zero measured quality degradation in the tested matrix; SGLang path documented as blocked by SM121 kernel compatibility.
featured.bounds: Single GPU, one target/draft model pair, modest sample sizes.

featured.title: GB10 Local Server Utility Swap Broker Validation
featured.why_it_matters: Targets practical home-lab inference operations: using brokered local endpoints without silently wedging the machine.
featured.result: The artifact documents a validation lane around local serving utility and memory/swap-aware broker behavior.
featured.bounds: Hardware- and configuration-specific; should be replicated before broad claims.

featured.title: Memory Pressure Admission Gate
featured.why_it_matters: Shows a queue/admission-control idea applied to LLM serving: compress or defer context-heavy prompts before they harm latency.
featured.result: On a compact live llama.cpp benchmark, the gate reported 69.0% lower p95 latency and 71.0% lower energy proxy with zero completion loss versus throughput-only baseline.
featured.bounds: Single server stack, one quantized model, 16-request deterministic workload.

featured.title: Value-per-Joule Broker Online Canary
featured.why_it_matters: Turns local inference routing into an explicit value-per-joule decision, which is exactly the kind of operational metric small AI labs need.
featured.result: Replay and live canary modes reported lower cost/joules per successful output without rollback-guard failures in the tested setting.
featured.bounds: Small models, single host, short-duration canary.

featured.title: Wake-Gate Local Endpoint Harness Integration
featured.why_it_matters: Connects model endpoint validation to the wake-gated dispatch system, showing how Enoch turns local endpoint checks into runnable project infrastructure.
featured.result: The contract harness successfully probed `/models` and `/chat/completions` on a live llama.cpp backend and shut the service down cleanly afterward.
featured.bounds: Functional probe only; no latency/throughput benchmark.

featured.title: Open-Weight Integrity Twin Agent Sweep
featured.why_it_matters: A useful example of the corpus exploring model/evaluator mismatch and tampering surfaces rather than only speed benchmarks.
featured.result: The artifact reports measurable evaluator-surface tampering in local open-weight endpoints with model-size-sensitive behavior.
featured.bounds: Small model panel and limited model-family coverage.
